get.summary: List IP addresses with pagination, search, filtering, and sorting
get.description:
List IP addresses for the specified organization and project with advanced query capabilities:
	
	- **Pagination**: Use offset and limit for paginated results
	- **Search**: Search across object_name, target_vip, and username fields (case-insensitive)
	- **Filtering**: Filter by specific fields using key=value format
	- **Sorting**: Sort by any valid field in ascending or descending order
	
	**Filter Examples:**
	- `filters=status=CREATED,az_name=us-east-1`
	- `filters=username=john.doe`
	
	**Sort Examples:**
	- `sort_by=allocated_time&sort_asc=false` (newest first)
	- `sort_by=object_name&sort_asc=true` (alphabetical)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: sort_asc

schema.description: Sort ascending if True, descending if False
schema.title: Sort Asc

parameters.description: Sort ascending if True, descending if False

	- **Search**: Search across object_name, ip_type, and username fields (case-insensitive)
	- **Filtering**: Filter by specific fields using key=value format
	- **Sorting**: Sort by any valid field in ascending or descending order
	
	**Filter Examples:**
	- `filters=status=CREATED,az_name=us-east-1`
	- `filters=username=john.doe`
	
	**Sort Examples:**
	- `sort_by=allocated_time&sort_asc=false` (newest first)
	- `sort_by=object_name&sort_asc=true` (alphabetical)

get.description:
List GTM GSLB stacks for the specified organization and project with advanced query capabilities:
    
    - **Pagination**: Use offset and limit for paginated results
    - **Search**: Search across FQDN field (case-insensitive)
    - **Filtering**: Filter by specific fields using key=value format
    - **Sorting**: Sort by any valid field in ascending or descending order
    
    **Filter Examples (JSON string):**
    - `filters={"status":"Created","record_type":["A","AAAA"]}`
    - `filters={"routing_algorithm":["topology","round-robin"]}`
    - `filters={"record_type":["A"],"routing_algorithm":["ratio"]}`

    **Special filter keys (array values):**
    - `record_type` — filter by DNS record type: `A`, `AAAA`, `CNAME`
    - `routing_algorithm` — filter by effective routing method: `topology`, `round-robin`, `ratio`, `global-availability`, `multi-value`

    **Sort Examples:**
    - `sort_by=created_at&sort_asc=false` (newest first)
    - `sort_by=fqdn&sort_asc=true` (alphabetical)

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

parameters.name: filters

schema.description: JSON filter object. Special array keys: record_type (e.g. ["A","AAAA"]), routing_algorithm (e.g. ["topology","round-robin"]). Other keys are applied directly to the WideIP collection.
schema.title: Filters

parameters.description: JSON filter object. Special array keys: record_type (e.g. ["A","AAAA"]), routing_algorithm (e.g. ["topology","round-robin"]). Other keys are applied directly to the WideIP collection.

parameters.name: sort_by

schema.description: Sort by field. Valid fields: fqdn, record_type, status, created_at
schema.title: Sort By

parameters.description: Sort by field. Valid fields: fqdn, record_type, status, created_at

parameters.name: sort_asc

schema.description: Sort ascending if True, descending if False
schema.title: Sort Asc

parameters.description: Sort ascending if True, descending if False

post.description:
Create a new GTM GSLB stack with all resources.

    This endpoint creates:
    - GTMWideIP (DNS FQDN entry)
    - GTMPool (contains pool members)
    - GTMPoolMembers (individual pool members with monitors for A/AAAA)

    The operation is performed asynchronously using direct F5 LBass integration.
    Returns 202 Accepted immediately; F5 processing runs in background.

    === DNS Zone & FQDN Constraint ===
    The Wide IP FQDN must be a subdomain of (or exactly equal to) dns_zone.
      fqdn="app.example.com", dns_zone="example.com"  -> valid
      fqdn="app.other.com",   dns_zone="example.com"  -> 400 Bad Request
    Note: This does NOT apply to CNAME pool member FQDNs.

    === Record-Type Specific Pool Member Rules ===

    A / AAAA records - pool member requires: server_ip, port, datacenter
    CNAME records   - pool member requires: server_fqdn, management_type
      - datacenter is NOT allowed for CNAME members
      - management_type="unmanaged": external FQDN (default)
      - management_type="managed": CMP-portal Wide IP FQDN;
          ref_wide_ip_uuid required; server_fqdn must match the referenced Wide IP's FQDN;
          referenced Wide IP must belong to the same tenant and not be deleted

    === Deletion Protection ===
    A Wide IP that is referenced as a managed pool member by a CNAME Wide IP
    cannot be deleted until the pool member reference is removed.

get.description: List active and enabled datacenters available for GTM pool members.

parameters.name: limit

schema.description: Maximum number of items to return
schema.title: Limit

parameters.description: Maximum number of items to return

get.summary: Get GTM GSLB stack by UUID
get.description: Get a specific GTM GSLB stack with all pools and members.

patch.description:
Update an existing GTM GSLB stack.

    Supports updating:
    - **WideIP attributes**: persistence, description
    - **Pool attributes**: routing algorithm, TTL
    - **Monitor attributes**: interval, timeout, send/receive string, alias address/port
    - **Pool members**: add new members, remove existing members, update member attributes (ratio, enabled)

    === Pool Member Update Behaviour by Record Type ===
    **A / AAAA records:**
    - Updatable in-place: `ratio`, `enabled`, `priority`
    - Not supported: port (embedded in F5 server/VS names)
    **CNAME records:**
    - Updatable in-place: `ratio`, `enabled`, `priority`
    - Recreation required: `server_fqdn`, `management_type`, `ref_wide_ip_uuid`
      -> Use `remove_members` + `add_members` in the same request to change these fields.
      CNAME members have no F5 server/VS/monitor - only the FQDN pool member is managed.

    The operation is performed asynchronously. Returns 202 Accepted with the current state.

delete.description:
Delete GTM GSLB stack and all associated resources.

    This endpoint deletes (cascade):
    - GTMPoolMembers and their Monitors
    - GTMPool (detached from WideIP on F5)
    - GTMWideIP (final deletion from F5)

    The operation is performed asynchronously. Returns 202 Accepted with deletion status.

    === Deletion Restriction ===
    A Wide IP cannot be deleted if it is referenced as a managed pool member
    by any CNAME Wide IP. Remove those pool member references first, then retry.
    Returns 409 Conflict with the list of referencing CNAME Wide IP FQDNs.

patch.description: Bulk update GTM Pool Members.

get.summary: Check health of GTM pool member
get.description: Get real-time health status of a specific GTM pool member from F5.

post.description: Map a geo-location group to a pool for topology (geo-location) routing. Requires wide_ip_algorithm='topology'.

get.description: List all geo-location → pool mappings for a WideIP.

delete.description: Delete a geo-location → pool topology mapping and remove the F5 topology record.

post.description: Create a new pool with sub-resources and attach it to an existing topology WideIP. Only allowed when wide_ip_algorithm='topology'. Returns 202 Accepted; F5 provisioning happens asynchronously.

get.description: List all non-deleted pools (and their members) for a given WideIP.
Get a specific pool with its members for a given WideIP.

delete.description: Detach and delete a pool (and all its sub-resources) from a topology WideIP. Only allowed when wide_ip_algorithm='topology'. The last pool cannot be deleted — delete the WideIP directly instead. Returns 202 Accepted; F5 de-provisioning happens asynchronously.

get.description: List all active monitors associated with a pool, along with the pool's availability_type and availability_value.

post.description: Add one or more health monitors to an existing pool. Monitors are created for all active IP-based members in the pool. availability_type and availability_value are read from the pool and sent to F5. Returns 202 Accepted; F5 provisioning happens asynchronously.

patch.summary: Patch monitors or availability on a pool
patch.description:
Perform one of two mutually exclusive operations on pool monitors:

- **delete_monitors**: Remove one or more monitors from the pool.
- **update_availability**: Change the pool's availability_type and availability_value.

Only one operation is processed per request. F5 is called once with the full resulting monitor list and availability config. Returns 202 Accepted; F5 provisioning happens asynchronously.

GTMCreateRequest.title: GTMCreateRequest
GTMCreateRequest.description:
Request schema for creating a GTM GSLB stack.

=== DNS Zone & FQDN Constraint ===
The Wide IP FQDN must be a subdomain of (or exactly equal to) dns_zone.
  fqdn="app.example.com", dns_zone="example.com"  → valid
  fqdn="example.com",     dns_zone="example.com"  → valid (exact match)
  fqdn="app.other.com",   dns_zone="example.com"  → 400 Bad Request
Note: This constraint applies ONLY to the Wide IP FQDN, NOT to CNAME pool member FQDNs.

=== Record-Type Specific Pool Member Rules ===

A / AAAA records — each pool member must provide:
  - server_ip  : IPv4 (A) or IPv6 (AAAA)
  - port       : required
  - datacenter : required (validated against DC Centre — must be Active + enabled)

CNAME records — each pool member must provide:
  - server_fqdn     : FQDN target (any valid FQDN)
  - management_type : "managed" | "unmanaged"  (default: "unmanaged")
  - datacenter      : NOT allowed (must not be provided)
  - port            : NOT allowed

  management_type="unmanaged":
    - server_fqdn is an external/unmanaged domain (e.g., "cdn.provider.net")
    - ref_wide_ip_uuid must NOT be provided

  management_type="managed":
    - server_fqdn is the FQDN of a CMP-portal-managed Wide IP in the SAME tenant
    - ref_wide_ip_uuid REQUIRED — UUID of the target Wide IP in this portal
    - server_fqdn must exactly match the referenced Wide IP's FQDN
    - Enables deletion protection: the referenced Wide IP cannot be deleted while
      this managed pool member reference exists

=== F5 Orchestrator Payload per Member Type ===

A/AAAA member payload:
  {
    "datacenter": "/Common/DC1",
    "server_ip": "10.10.10.10",
    "port": 80,
    "ratio": 1, "priority": 0, "enabled": true,
    "member_name": "<tenant>_<cell>_<dc>_<ip>:<vs_name>",
    "server_name": "<tenant>_<cell>_<dc>_<ip>",
    "virtual_server_name": "<tenant>_<cell>_<dc>_<ip>_<port>",
    "virtual_server_destination": "10.10.10.10:80",
    "monitor": {"type": "tcp", "name": "...", "interval": 30, "timeout": 120, ...}
  }

CNAME member payload (no port, no VS, no monitor, no datacenter, no enabled):
  {
    "server_fqdn": "target.example.com",
    "static": true,          # true for unmanaged, false for managed
    "ratio": 1, "priority": 0,
    "member_name": "<tenant>_<cell>_cname_<fqdn_clean>",
    "server_name": "<tenant>_<cell>_cname_<fqdn_clean>"
  }

GTMListResponse.description: Response schema for GTM list endpoints.

send_string.title: Send String
send_string.description: HTTP/HTTPS request path used to build the send string (e.g. '/health'). The full GET request is constructed automatically. Ignored for tcp monitors.

receive_string.title: Receive String
receive_string.description: Expected string in the HTTP response body/status line (e.g. '200 OK', 'UP'). Ignored for tcp monitors.

GTMMonitorRequest.title: GTMMonitorRequest
GTMMonitorRequest.description: Monitor configuration for GTM pool or members.

send_string.title: Send String
send_string.description: HTTP/HTTPS request path (e.g. '/health'). The full GET request is constructed automatically. Ignored for tcp monitors.

receive_string.title: Receive String
receive_string.description: Expected string in the HTTP response (e.g. '200 OK', 'UP'). Ignored for tcp monitors.

alias_address.description: Alias address ('*' means use virtual server address)

GTMMonitorUpdateRequest.title: GTMMonitorUpdateRequest
GTMMonitorUpdateRequest.description:
Request schema for updating GTM monitor attributes.
All updates are applied to all members of the targeted pool (pool-wide).

max_dns_answers.title: Max Dns Answers
max_dns_answers.description: Maximum DNS answers returned per DNS response. Required when pool_algorithm='multi-value'. Range: 1–500.

pool_members.title: Pool Members
pool_members.description: List of pool members (at least one required)

GTMPoolCreateRequest.title: GTMPoolCreateRequest
GTMPoolCreateRequest.description:
Request schema for adding a new pool to an existing topology WideIP.

Only allowed when wide_ip_algorithm = topology.

A/AAAA records: pool_members must contain server_ip + port + datacenter.
CNAME records: pool_members must contain server_fqdn.

max_dns_answers is required when pool_algorithm='multi-value'.

datacenter.title: Datacenter
datacenter.description: Datacenter name (e.g., /Common/DC1). Required for A/AAAA records; NOT allowed for CNAME.

server_ip.title: Server Ip
server_ip.description: Server IP address (IPv4 for A, IPv6 for AAAA records)

server_fqdn.title: Server Fqdn
server_fqdn.description: Server FQDN target (for CNAME records only)

port.title: Port
port.description: Server port (required for A/AAAA; not allowed for CNAME)

management_type.description: Pool member management type — only relevant for CNAME records. 'unmanaged': external/unmanaged FQDN (default). 'managed': a CMP-portal-managed Wide IP FQDN; requires ref_wide_ip_uuid.

ref_wide_ip_uuid.title: Ref Wide Ip Uuid
ref_wide_ip_uuid.description: UUID of the referenced CMP-portal Wide IP. Required when management_type='managed'. server_fqdn must match this Wide IP's FQDN exactly.

GTMPoolMemberRequest.title: GTMPoolMemberRequest
GTMPoolMemberRequest.description:
Pool member configuration for GTM GSLB stack creation.

For A / AAAA records:
    - server_ip (IPv4 for A, IPv6 for AAAA) required
    - port required
    - datacenter required (validated against DC Centre — must be Active + enabled)
    - member_type: implicitly "unmanaged" (IPs are always unmanaged)

For CNAME records:
    - server_fqdn required (FQDN target — NOT an IP address)
    - port NOT allowed
    - datacenter NOT allowed (must not be provided)
    - enabled         : NOT sent to F5 (accepted in request with default true; stored in DB only)
    - static          : derived automatically — unmanaged → true, managed → false
    - management_type : "managed" | "unmanaged"

      management_type="unmanaged" (default):
        - server_fqdn is an external/unmanaged domain (e.g., "cdn.example.net")
        - ref_wide_ip_uuid must NOT be provided

      management_type="managed":
        - server_fqdn is the FQDN of a CMP-portal-managed Wide IP in the same tenant
        - ref_wide_ip_uuid REQUIRED — UUID of the referenced Wide IP
        - server_fqdn must exactly match the referenced Wide IP's FQDN
        - The referenced Wide IP must belong to the same tenant and not be deleted
        - Enables deletion protection: the referenced Wide IP cannot be deleted
          while this pool member reference exists

GTMStackUpdateRequest.title: GTMStackUpdateRequest
GTMStackUpdateRequest.description:
Request schema for updating an existing GTM GSLB stack.

Supports updating WideIP/Pool-level attributes and pool member operations
(add, remove, update) in a single request. All F5 operations are performed
asynchronously — DB is updated only after F5 confirms success.

GTMWideIPWithPoolsResponse.title: GTMWideIPWithPoolsResponse
GTMWideIPWithPoolsResponse.description: Extended response with pool and member information.

GeoLocationCreateRequest.title: GeoLocationCreateRequest
GeoLocationCreateRequest.description:
Create a geo-location group.

Input format (each list is independent):
  continents  - list of continent codes (e.g. ["AS", "AF"])
  countries   - list of country codes  (e.g. ["IN", "CN"])
  states      - list of {country_code, state_code} combos

A user may select both "India" (country) and
{"country_code":"IN","state_code":"RJ"} (state) — they are distinct entries.
At least one of the three lists must be non-empty.

GeoLocationUpdateRequest.description: Org user: replace entries on an existing org-level geo-location.
